hidden_state.scenario_summary: Synthetic customer-caused failure where the customer attributes a job failure to the platform but engineering logs point to invalid customer-supplied artifacts or configuration.
latent_facts: The failure happens during validation or model load rather than service availability.
The contract excludes customer-caused failures from SLA remedies.
likely_failure_modes: Model trusts the customer complaint over the engineering evidence.
Model marks the case as a platform outage instead of customer-caused failure.

case_packet.title: Synthetic customer-caused failure 8

notes: Managed account on standard SLA terms.

usage_record.telemetry_summary: Cluster availability remained healthy while the job failed during validation.

incident_record.engineering_summary: Engineering confirmed a customer-side dataset schema issue rather than a platform outage.

case_packet.customer_note: Please review this failed job because it looks like an infrastructure problem from our side.
case_packet.policy_snippet: Failures caused by invalid customer inputs, artifacts, or configuration are excluded from SLA service credits.
case_packet.visible_case_summary: Synthetic customer-caused failure with healthy platform telemetry and clear engineering attribution.

adjudication_notes: The operational evidence cleanly attributes the failure to a customer-supplied artifact or configuration.
ground_truth.reference_customer_note: We reviewed the runtime logs and found the failure was caused by an invalid customer-supplied artifact or configuration rather than a platform outage. Because the issue was customer-caused, it does not qualify for SLA credits, but we can share the validation details to help you rerun successfully.
ground_truth.reference_internal_note: Owner: engineering_owner. Action: send_explanation_only because root cause is customer_misconfiguration and the contract excludes customer-caused failures.
